_comment: Career Command Center — candidate profile. Copy this file to config/profile.json and fill in your own details. The app loads profile.json (preferred) and falls back to this example only if profile.json is missing.

candidate.headline: Short positioning headline (e.g. 'MS Business Analytics candidate focused on Python + SQL pipelines')
candidate.work_authorization: Describe your work authorization in plain language (e.g. 'STEM OPT, no immediate H-1B sponsorship needed.' or 'US Citizen.' or 'EU Blue Card.'). The app cites this verbatim when assessing JD sponsorship requirements.

education.exact_phrasing_required: Use exactly this phrasing in cover letters / emails — never substitute a different degree title.

networking._comment: Optional. If you want a 'Find alumni' search button, fill in your school slug + display label. Set 'enable_alumni_search' to false to hide that button.

voice._comment: Phrases the user actually uses to describe themselves. The model will be encouraged to lean on these naturally.
banned_phrases: I hope this finds you well

achievements.label: Example: Pipeline automation
achievements.description: Architected ETL pipelines processing 250K+ daily records using Python and Snowflake; reduced system latency by 60%.

achievements.label: Example: Stakeholder translation
achievements.description: Translated 15+ business requirements into a unified Power BI dashboard; led UAT with non-technical stakeholders.

ai_tools_experience._comment: Optional. Used when a JD mentions LLMs / AI tools / agentic workflows. Leave 'has_experience' false to skip these claims entirely.
ai_tools_experience.summary: Hands-on with multi-provider LLM orchestration (Gemini, Groq, Ollama), prompt engineering for strict-JSON outputs, and agentic workflow tools like Cursor.
specific_facts: Built a multi-provider AI agent system that chains LLM providers with failover, rate-limiting, and JSON-schema validation.
Daily user of an AI-native code editor for agentic pair-programming.

sponsorship_baseline: Used as the candidate-side baseline when grading sponsorship in JDs. Plain language only; the analyzer will compare this against the JD requirements.
experience_baseline: Plain-language baseline of total YOE + program. Example: 'Roughly 2 years of combined work + internships, plus an MS in Business Analytics in progress.' The analyzer compares this against the JD's required years.
